title: Migration 010: retrospection + evolution schema columns and schema.ts updates
acceptance_criteria: 010_retrospection_evolution.sql adds M2 columns to personality_configs defensively using ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS: display_name TEXT; group_type TEXT CHECK (group_type IN ('reference', 'learning')); params JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'

Data migration: for rows where params = '{}'::jsonb (M1 rows not yet migrated), populate params from top-level M1 columns: UPDATE personality_configs SET params = jsonb_build_object('min_probability', min_probability, 'max_daily_loss', max_daily_loss_pct, 'max_daily_trades', 5) WHERE params = '{}'::jsonb AND min_probability IS NOT NULL
Set display_name and group_type for the existing Clockwork row: UPDATE personality_configs SET display_name='Clockwork', group_type='reference' WHERE name='Clockwork' AND display_name IS NULL
Add evolution tracking columns to personality_configs using ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS: last_evolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ; evolution_consecutive_applications INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
Add new metric columns to retrospection_results using ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS: sharpe NUMERIC; max_drawdown_pct NUMERIC; proposed_adjustments_at TIMESTAMPTZ
All ALTER TABLE statements use ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS — the migration is fully idempotent and safe to re-run on an already-migrated database
No new hypertables — retrospection_results is and remains a regular PostgreSQL table (not a hypertable); do NOT call create_hypertable() on it
No DROP, RENAME, or modification of any existing column in any table — additive-only changes

src/db/schema.ts: add sharpe: number | null, max_drawdown_pct: number | null, proposed_adjustments_at: Date | null to the RetrospectionResult interface (these three fields match the new retrospection_results columns; NUMERIC → number | null, TIMESTAMPTZ → Date | null following existing schema conventions)

src/db/schema.ts: add last_evolved_at: Date | null, evolution_consecutive_applications: number to the PersonalityConfigM2 interface (camelCase convention used by that interface; these match the two new personality_configs columns; TIMESTAMPTZ → Date | null, INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 → number)

src/db/schema.ts: do NOT add display_name / group_type / params to the PersonalityConfig interface (the snake_case M1 interface) — those are already mapped in PersonalityConfigM2; adding duplicates would cause confusion. Add a code comment on PersonalityConfig referencing PersonalityConfigM2 for M2 fields if one is not already present
The migration file must begin with a comment block stating its purpose: '-- Migration 010: Add retrospection metric columns, evolution tracking columns, and M2 personality params migration'
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
